title: Aria and the Mystery Doodle
description: Theo draws a picture and asks Aria to guess what it is. She gets it wrong — and that turns out to be the most useful part of the game.

steps.content: Theo drew a round shape with two triangles on top. Byte rolled forward, screen blinking. 'A cat! It must be a cat!' Byte chirped. 'Nope,' Theo grinned. 'It's a slice of pizza. Those are pepperoni.'

steps.question: Why do you think Byte guessed 'cat' instead of 'pizza'?
options: Byte was trying to trick Theo
Round shapes with triangles usually look like cat ears to a robot
Byte does not like pepperoni pizza

steps.content: Aria's lights flickered warmly. 'You guessed from a pattern, Byte. In your memory, round things with triangles are usually cats. But patterns can trick us. What do you think happens if we guess too quickly?' Byte's screen drooped. 'We sound sure, but we are wrong!'

steps.instruction: Theo drew something new: three stacked circles. Byte paused, loading dots dancing on his screen. 'Is it a snowman? A robot? A tower? I don't know!' What is the best way to help Byte learn?
options: Tell Byte to guess anyway and sound sure
Ask Byte why he sees a snowman, and then tell him it's a sandcastle
Tell Byte that robots shouldn't make guesses
Turn Byte off and reset his memory

steps.content: Theo smiled. 'It's a sandcastle!' Byte's antenna perked up happily. 'I was only 30% sure it was a snowman. Next time, I will tell you how sure I am!' Aria glowed softly. 'Exactly, Byte. Sounding sure and being right are not the same. It is good to say when we aren't sure.'
